question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator call touchMarket with market.maturity = 0 (or any value < block.timestamp), since the only maturity check is the upper-bound require(market.maturity <= block.timestamp + 100 * 365 days, MaturityTooFar()), creating a market where block.timestamp > market.maturity is immediately true, violating the invariant that post-maturity liquidation mode should only activate after the agreed lending period, causing scoped impact: all positions in the malformed market are immediately post-maturity liquidatable at LIF = maxLif (since block.timestamp - 0 >> TIME_TO_MAX_LIF), enabling instant seizure of any borrower collateral? Proof idea: deploy market with maturity=0, supply collateral, take a buy offer to create debt, immediately call liquidate with postMaturityMode=true and assert seizedAssets > 0 at full maxLif.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.rcfThreshold = type(uint256).max, since touchMarket performs no validation on rcfThreshold, violating the invariant that the recovery close factor must cap normal-mode liquidations to the amount needed to restore health, causing scoped impact: the RCF dust-bypass condition _position.collateral[collateralIndex].mulDivDown(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, lif).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) < market.rcfThreshold is always true (collateral value is bounded by uint128), so any unhealthy position can be fully liquidated in normal mode regardless of how close to health it is? Proof idea: create market with rcfThreshold=type(uint256).max, create a position that is 1 unit below maxDebt, call liquidate with repaidUnits = position.debt and assert it succeeds instead of reverting with RecoveryCloseFactorConditionsViolated.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.rcfThreshold = 0, since touchMarket performs no validation on rcfThreshold, violating the invariant that dust positions below the economic liquidation threshold must remain liquidatable to prevent bad debt accumulation, causing scoped impact: the RCF dust-bypass condition ... < 0 is always false (uint256 is never negative), so for any unhealthy position where repaidUnits > maxRepaid the liquidation always reverts with RecoveryCloseFactorConditionsViolated, making small undercollateralized positions permanently unliquidatable and accumulating bad debt? Proof idea: create market with rcfThreshold=0, create a position with collateral value just below rcfThreshold dust threshold, make it unhealthy, call liquidate with repaidUnits = position.debt and assert it reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.loanToken = address(0), since touchMarket performs no validation on loanToken, violating the invariant that every market must have a valid ERC-20 loan token, causing scoped impact: any subsequent take, repay, withdraw, or claimContinuousFee call that invokes SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(address(0), ...) or safeTransferFrom(address(0), ...) will either silently succeed (if address(0) has no code) or revert unpredictably, corrupting accounting by updating credit/debt state before the transfer check? Proof idea: create market with loanToken=address(0), call touchMarket to confirm it succeeds, then call take and assert the accounting state is updated while the token transfer silently no-ops.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / isHealthy / liquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator set collateralParams[i].oracle = address(0) or any non-contract address, since touchMarket performs no validation on oracle addresses, violating the invariant that health checks and liquidations must be able to query collateral prices, causing scoped impact: any call to isHealthy, withdrawCollateral (when borrower has debt), or liquidate that iterates the collateralBitmap and calls IOracle(address(0)).price() will revert with no code at address, permanently freezing all health-dependent operations for borrowers in that market? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / supplyCollateral / liquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator set collateralParams[i].token = market.loanToken (same address for loan and collateral token), since touchMarket performs no check that collateralToken != loanToken, violating the invariant that loan token balances and collateral token balances must be independently tracked, causing scoped impact: a borrower who supplies loanToken as collateral and then borrows loanToken creates a position where the same ERC-20 balance is double-counted as both collateral and withdrawable, enabling extraction of more loanToken than was deposited? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.maturity = 0, causing updatePositionView to compute accrualEnd = UtilsLib.min(block.timestamp, 0) = 0 and the condition _lastAccrual < market.maturity to evaluate as _lastAccrual < 0 which is always false for uint128, violating the invariant that continuous fees must accrue proportionally over the lending period, causing scoped impact: continuous fees never accrue in this market regardless of continuousFee setting, so the fee claimer can never claim continuousFeeCredit and lenders effectively pay zero continuous fees? Proof idea: create market with maturity=0 and nonzero continuousFee, take an offer to create credit, warp time forward, call updatePosition and assert accruedFee == 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / take] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.maturity = block.timestamp (exactly at creation time), since the debt-increase guard is require(block.timestamp <= offer.market.maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0, CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity()) which uses <=, meaning debt CAN increase at exactly maturity, but post-maturity liquidation requires block.timestamp > market.maturity which is false at exactly maturity, violating the invariant that the maturity boundary must not allow debt increase and post-maturity liquidation to be simultaneously unavailable, causing scoped impact: a borrower can increase debt at exactly maturity but cannot be post-maturity liquidated at that same timestamp, creating a window where new debt is created in a market that is effectively expired? Proof idea: set block.timestamp = maturity, call take with sellerDebtIncrease > 0 and assert it succeeds, then call liquidate with postMaturityMode=true and assert it reverts with NotLiquidatable.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / liquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.maturity = 1 (1 second past Unix epoch), since there is no lower bound on maturity, causing block.timestamp - market.maturity >> TIME_TO_MAX_LIF (15 minutes) to always be true, violating the invariant that post-maturity LIF must ramp linearly from 1 to maxLif over TIME_TO_MAX_LIF, causing scoped impact: the LIF computation lif = min(_maxLif, WAD + (_maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - market.maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF) immediately saturates to maxLif for any realistic block.timestamp, so liquidators always receive maximum incentive from the first second, enabling maximum collateral seizure with no ramp-up period? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / IdLib.storeInCode] Can an unprivileged attacker pre-compute the CREATE2 address for a target market's id (computed as keccak256(abi.encodePacked(uint8(0xff), midnight, chainId, keccak256(abi.encodePacked(SSTORE2_PREFIX, abi.encode(market)))))) and deploy a contract at that address before touchMarket is called, since storeInCode uses create2 with chainId as salt and requires create2Address != address(0), violating the invariant that any valid market must be creatable by any caller, causing scoped impact: the require(create2Address != address(0), SStore2DeploymentFailed()) check fails because CREATE2 returns address(0) when the target address is already occupied, permanently DoS-ing creation of that specific market? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / liquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.liquidatorGate to a contract that always returns false or reverts for canLiquidate, since touchMarket performs no validation on liquidatorGate, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable to prevent bad debt accumulation, causing scoped impact: all calls to liquidate revert with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating, making every borrower position in the market permanently unliquidatable regardless of health, allowing borrowers to borrow without risk of liquidation? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / take] Can an unprivileged market creator set market.enterGate to a contract that selectively returns false for canIncreaseDebt for specific addresses but true for others, since touchMarket performs no validation on enterGate, violating the invariant that gate checks must apply uniformly to all market participants, causing scoped impact: the attacker can create a market where only whitelisted addresses can borrow, effectively creating a private lending market that appears public but silently blocks debt increases for non-whitelisted takers? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IPermit2.sol -> Scope: High Market creation parameter bug allowing malformed markets, unsorted collateral lists, duplicate collateral, invalid LLTV, invalid LIF, or unsafe maturity values'] [Function: touchMarket / repay / take] Can an unprivileged market creator create a market with maturity in the past (maturity < block.timestamp), then call repay with units > 0 on a position with zero debt, since repay does not check that position.debt >= units before subtracting (position[id][onBehalf].debt -= UtilsLib.toUint128(units) would underflow and revert in Solidity 0.8), but the marketState.withdrawable += units still executes if the subtraction succeeds, violating the invariant that withdrawable must only increase by actual repaid debt, causing scoped impact: if a borrower has exactly units debt, repay succeeds and withdrawable increases, but in a past-maturity market this creates withdrawable credit that lenders can immediately withdraw?
